_comment: Static FIXTURE shape catalog — immovable building pieces AND non-disguisable decoration (appliances, counters, tables, shelves, plus food/cookware/dishes that sit ON surfaces as fixed scenery). Kept SEPARATE from props.json on purpose: props.json is the disguise catalog (only genuinely movable items a player can hide as), and a fixture must never be disguisable. Fixtures are referenced ONLY from a map's fixtures[] array; the referee builds its disguise pool from map.props alone, so nothing here can ever become a disguise or hit the network broadcast — fixtures render from each client's LOCAL maps.json. That is why 'fill the restaurant with more stuff' is free here: adding hundreds of fixtures costs zero bandwidth and never widens the disguise pool. Each entry keeps a primitive shape (fallback + GLB size target) plus a `model` GLB path; `modelSize` overrides the auto size target (largest bounding dimension), and `modelDims`:{w,h,d} scales the GLB per-axis to EXACT world sizes (non-uniform) for pieces whose native proportions don't match the intended footprint (floor tiles). scene.js merges this with props.json into one render lookup at match start.
_comment_flags: FIXED/DYNAMIC (the knockable-world rule). FLOATING-FIXED-PROPS ROUND 4 (2026-07-17, VRmike): the ONE rule for a FIXED, immovable collider is now physics.isFixedBodyEntry = world ARCHITECTURE (`arch`) OR wall-attached (`wallAttached`). Nothing else is ever fixed. The genuinely-fixed set: ARCHITECTURE — floor_kitchen/kitchen_wall/wall_post/wall_header (all `arch`); WALL-ATTACHED (`wallAttached`) — the structural PILLARS (pillar/pillar_b, 3.08 m floor-to-ceiling columns), the DOOR (set in its frame) and the VENT/extractor hood (mounted high above the stove). EVERYTHING ELSE is DYNAMIC/knockable: the large BUILT-INS (counters, cabinets, oven, stove(s), fridge, sinks, shelf) AND all the tabletop clutter (tables, plates, pots, pans, dishes, food, condiments, canisters, dishrack, planks, towels). They are real shovable rigid bodies that FALL (a fridge nudges heavy, a plate skitters light — mass = collider volume x propDensity). NOTE the legacy `"static": true` flag is kept on the fixed pieces for backward-compat but is NO LONGER the classifier's input (isFixedBodyEntry reads arch/wallAttached); a `static` flag alone would NOT make a piece fixed. There is NO y-threshold "pin" any more — that froze surface decor in mid-air (this round's bug). Surface clutter is SEATED on the collider actually beneath it at load (shared/grounding.js seatMapData) so it never spawns launching out of a tall hull. Phone budget: rules.maxDynamicProps caps how many are truly dynamic; the referee sorts BIGGEST-FIRST so the big built-ins win dynamic bodies and only the tiniest food scraps degrade to a fixed-but-collidable (and already-seated, so resting-not-floating) static collider. `"floor": true` additionally tells physics to thicken the collider ~1m DOWNWARD while keeping the visible top flush (fix #5). Absent flag => dynamic — the world defaults to knockable.
_comment_wallattached: WALL-ATTACHED (`wallAttached`, floating-fixed-props round 4). Marks the non-architecture pieces that must stay bolted to the walls/architecture even in an all-dynamic world: the DOOR, the VENT/extractor hood, and the structural PILLARS. Kept DELIBERATELY SEPARATE from the disguise classifier (isDisguisableEntry reads `shape && !arch`) so a door/vent stays BOTH disguisable (a player may dress as it) AND immovable — the physics system reads `wallAttached`, the disguise system reads its own list, and neither can rewrite the other. See physics.isWallAttachedEntry/isFixedBodyEntry.
_comment_arch: DISGUISE-ANYTHING (Part B): a player may disguise as ANY object EXCEPT world ARCHITECTURE. `"arch": true` marks the architecture pieces that stay OFF-LIMITS — floors, boundary/room walls, wall panels/dividers, ceilings. Only floor_kitchen + the wall pieces (kitchen_wall, wall_post, wall_header) carry it. EVERYTHING ELSE here is disguisable: the now-DYNAMIC built-ins (counters, cabinets, oven, stove, fridge, sinks, shelf) and the still-static PILLARS, DOOR and vent/extractor hood alike — the referee promotes each into the prop stream as an aim-target and setPlayerCollider grows the disguised player's capsule to its footprint. Disguise is orthogonal to static/dynamic (isDisguisableEntry reads `shape && !arch`, never `static`), so a disguised player is kinematic and firm whether the real object they mimic is a shovable dynamic body or a bolted-in static one. See physics.isArchEntry / isDisguisableEntry and referee.startMatch.
_comment_floor: floor_kitchen: measured native tile = 4 x 0.5 x 4 (the 0.5 native THICKNESS x scale was the raised-podium bug). modelDims forces it non-uniformly to a wide, near-flush 8 x 0.06 x 8 slab that rests flush at y=0 — walkable at ground level, no step. The primitive h matches (0.06). `floor:true` makes the physics collider a ~1m-thick slab extended DOWNWARD (visible top stays at the same height) so a fast/falling body can't tunnel through the thin veneer (fix #5). modelDims takes precedence over map.modelScale for this one intentional non-uniform case.
_comment_structure: --- Structural: walls, pillars, door, and the interior kitchen/dining divider wall (all static).
_comment_wall: kitchen_wall: modular_walls.glb is a multi-panel KIT (several wall variants spread across ~12 units), not one wall — placed as a single fixture and scaled it sprawled/shrank. Dropped the model; a clean full-height primitive box reads as a solid wall and already IS the collider. See asset-dims.json _kits.

door._noGround: mounted architecture-adjacent piece — never re-grounded (a door is set in its frame, not resting on a surface).

_comment_divider: Interior kitchen/dining DIVIDER WALL with pass-through SERVICE WINDOWS (fix #7). No single wall-with-window GLB exists in the pack (modular_walls is an unusable multi-panel kit — see asset-dims.json _kits), so per the approved plan the wall is built from plain static box fixtures at true wall height (~2.8u): the existing divider COUNTERS act as the waist-high window sills (service ledges facing the dining room), `wall_post` verticals frame the window bays floor-to-header, and `wall_header` lintels close the top of each bay — leaving open service windows between sill and header, plus clear walkway gaps. Hand-set sizes, eyeball-checked against door (2.1u) + player (1.8u) height; NOT routed through the empty measured-dims path. Windows face +z (the dining room).
_comment_appliances: --- Kitchen appliances (back wall). NOW DYNAMIC/shovable (2026-07-16 — every non-wall/door/vent object is a physics body): oven/stove(s)/fridge/cabinets are unflagged, so they get real rigid bodies and nudge like appliances. Only the extractor hood stays static (vent). Primitive dims = measured native x 0.75 (the map-wide modelScale), so the physics collider matches the drawn mesh. Unmeasured pieces (stove_plain/single, cabinet_corner, extractor) keep proportionate estimates and get the same 0.75 visual scale.

stove_plain._noHull: stove.glb's baked hull is a degenerate 0.20 m (vs a ~0.9 m stove), so a pot rests 0.7 m inside thin air and the newly-dynamic stove sits wrong — use the symmetric 0.9 m primitive box collider instead (floating-fixed-props round 4).

extractor._noGround: the VENT/extractor hood — wall-mounted high above the stove (y=1.9). Exempt from ground-snapping (plan: vents stay put), so removing the stove below it never drops it to the floor.

_comment_counters: --- Counters, sinks, shelves (NOW DYNAMIC/shovable — unflagged as of 2026-07-16; were static built-ins). Seated ON their floor: the kitchen sits on a 0.06m-raised floor_kitchen tile, so every kitchen fixture carries y=0.06 (base flush on the tile top) — a counter's bottom face is now at the tile surface a disguised player stands on, so a counter disguise matches the real counter's height exactly (VRmike's sunken-counter fix). 'counter' now uses kitchen_cabinet.glb (a single base cabinet at hip height): modular_kitchen_parts.glb is a 12-module KIT that shrank to an ankle-height blob when placed as one fixture (see asset-dims.json _kits). Sinks keep their taller (~1.35) bound because the native model includes the faucet; the working surface sits ~0.7. NOTE: the dishrack and TABLES are NOT here-static — a dishrack and the round/kitchen/bar tables are all knockable (see fix #1); only counters/cabinets/sinks/shelf are bolted-in.

shelf._noHull: shelf_papertowel.glb's baked hull has an OFF-CENTRE mass (the towels sit to one side), so as a dynamic body it tips itself over the instant the match starts — use the symmetric primitive box collider instead so it stays upright and shovable (floating-fixed-props round 4).

_comment_tables: --- Tables + racks (KNOCKABLE — no static flag). Per fix #1 the tables are now dynamic bodies so a shoved table settles with the clutter resting on it. round_table (dining), kitchen_table / table_food (kitchen prep), the two TALL bar tables (large_table/small_table), and the dishrack.
_comment_bartables: large_table (table.glb) and small_table (table_round_a_small.glb) are TALL bar/bistro tables in the pack (native ~1.93 / ~1.8 tall) — not dining height. Kept at their measured x0.75 (~1.45 / ~1.35); food on them sits high accordingly. Knockable.
_comment_cookware: --- Cookware & dishes placed ON surfaces (KNOCKABLE — no static flag; given a `y` in the map so they rest on a table/counter top and can be shoved off).
_comment_food: --- Prepared / raw food placed ON surfaces (KNOCKABLE — no static flag). None disguisable (they render via the non-disguisable side of the prop stream).
